key_insight: An exact hyperfine-relaxation model shows that localized carrier spins in metal-halide perovskites relax through different nuclear channels, with electrons mainly coupled to halogens and holes to metal nuclei.
engineering_relevance: This provides a way to predict and tune spin lifetimes in perovskites by choosing lighter halogens and metal cations, which is useful for spintronic and optoelectronic device design.
method: Adapted a muon spin relaxation framework to carrier spin dynamics.
Solved longitudinal spin relaxation exactly across arbitrary hyperfine correlation times.
Applied the model to time-resolved Faraday rotation data to extract localization volume and correlation time.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports fits to time-resolved Faraday rotation data from two perovskite samples, where the exact solution outperformed a mono-exponential approximation and enabled parameter extraction.
watch_item: The model is calibrated on localized-carrier measurements, so its applicability may be limited for delocalized transport regimes or samples with strong structural heterogeneity.
paper_title: Hyperfine driven spin relaxation of charge carriers in metal-halide perovskites
